key_insight: Chemical disorder and local chemical order in WTaCrV create lattice distortion that can offset radiation damage and preserve strength better than a homogenized average-atom model.
engineering_relevance: For irradiation-tolerant reactor materials, the study suggests that controlling nanoscale chemical structure may be as important as composition for maintaining mechanical integrity under extreme damage.
method: Atomistic simulations of collision cascades in WTaCrV using newly developed interatomic potentials
Comparison of Average-Atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO) configurations
Evaluation of Frenkel pair production and post-irradiation mechanical response
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that Frenkel pair generation follows NRSS > NLCO > NAA at the same dose, while irradiation severely degrades AA but has limited impact on strength and flow stress in RSS and LCO.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on atomistic modeling choices and newly developed potentials, so transferability to experimental microstructures and reactor operating conditions should be validated.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
